rationale.R1.1: The contribution claim (bias-free LLM judge for MT) and gap statement (self-preference bias in LLM-as-judge) need sharper articulation to carry the section's argumentative load.
rationale.R1.2: The core inferential leap — 0.3-point gap between GPT-4 and Claude scores entails absence of self-preference bias — is load-bearing and currently underargued; the logical chain from this single datum to the conclusion needs strengthening.
rationale.R1.3: Three quantitative claims (Spearman correlations, per-system score gap, cost figure) must be tightly coupled to the experimental setup described; any looseness here directly weakens the 'stronger' goal.
rationale.R1.6: Prose-level strengthening — tighter topic sentences, more assertive hedging-to-confidence calibration, and elimination of weak connectives ('these results have a clear implication') — is central to making the section feel authoritative.

parsed.clarification_question: Should 'make this stronger' prioritize (A) rhetorical/argumentative force — sharpening the thesis, tightening logical steps, and making claims land more confidently — or (B) empirical robustness — identifying gaps in the experimental design, missing baselines, or threats to validity that need to be addressed or disclosed?
